featureName: Sprint 19-21 — Edge Cases, Confirmations & Silent Failures
description: Fix silent failures, add missing confirmation dialogs for destructive actions, harden form validations for service listings, and improve home screen reliability for both Client and Provider roles.

userStories.title: Home Screen Sections — Show Loading & Error States Instead of Blank Space
userStories.description: JobOffersSection, PendingReviewsSection, and the provider's ServiceListingsSection all return SizedBox.shrink() for both loading and error states. Users see blank gaps with no indication that data is loading or failed. This is confusing — users think there's nothing there rather than a failure.
acceptanceCriteria: In JobOffersSection (home/presentation/widgets/job_offers_section.dart), replace loading SizedBox.shrink() with a shimmer placeholder or CircularProgressIndicator
In JobOffersSection, replace error SizedBox.shrink() with the existing ErrorState widget from core/widgets/error_state.dart, passing a retry callback that invalidates the provider
Apply the same loading + error treatment to PendingReviewsSection (home/presentation/widgets/pending_reviews_section.dart)
Apply the same loading + error treatment to the services listing section in ProviderHomeScreen (home/presentation/screens/provider_home_screen.dart)
Verify: kill Firestore emulator mid-session — all three sections show error state with retry button, not blank space
flutter analyze 0 errors

userStories.title: Confirmation Dialogs for Bid Accept & Reject Actions
userStories.description: Accepting or rejecting a bid is a significant, irreversible workflow action, but both happen on a single tap with no confirmation. A poster could accidentally reject the best bid or accept the wrong one.
acceptanceCriteria: Before accepting a bid, show an AlertDialog: 'Accept this bid for $X from [provider name]? This will assign them to your task.' with Cancel and Accept buttons
Before rejecting a bid, show an AlertDialog: 'Reject this bid from [provider name]? They will be notified.' with Cancel and Reject buttons
The Reject button should use a red/destructive color style
While the accept/reject API call is in progress, show a loading indicator on the confirmation button and disable both buttons
If the API call fails, show an error SnackBar and keep the dialog open
flutter analyze 0 errors
Write widget test: tap accept button, verify dialog appears, confirm acceptance

userStories.title: Enforce Account Deletion Confirmation — Require Typing DELETE
userStories.description: The account deletion flow in SettingsScreen shows a dialog that says 'Type DELETE to confirm' but the confirmation button is always enabled — user can tap it without typing anything. This defeats the safety mechanism.
acceptanceCriteria: In settings_screen.dart, add a TextField to the second confirmation dialog where user must type 'DELETE'
The 'Delete My Account' button in the dialog must be disabled until the TextField value exactly matches 'DELETE' (case-sensitive)
Show helper text below the field: 'Type DELETE to confirm'
Clear the TextField each time the dialog opens
The delete button should show a loading indicator while the deletion is in progress
flutter analyze 0 errors
Write widget test: open delete dialog, verify button is disabled, type DELETE, verify button becomes enabled

userStories.title: Service Listing Validation — Require At Least One Rate & Validate Ranges
userStories.description: CreateServiceScreen allows providers to submit a service listing with no hourly rate and no fixed rate set. Rate fields also accept negative numbers or absurdly high values. This creates broken listings that clients can't evaluate.
acceptanceCriteria: On form submission in CreateServiceScreen, validate that at least one of hourlyRate or fixedRate is provided — show SnackBar error if both are empty
Add validators to both rate TextFields: must be a positive number if filled, minimum $1, maximum $10,000
Show inline validation errors below rate fields (not just SnackBar)
Add currency input formatter (digits + one decimal point + max 2 decimal places) to both rate fields, matching the pattern used in SubmitBidSheet
Title field: require minimum 5 characters. Description field: require minimum 20 characters
flutter analyze 0 errors

userStories.title: Case-Insensitive Skill Tag Deduplication on Profile
userStories.description: EditProfileScreen's _addTag() uses case-sensitive .contains() check. A provider can add both '#DogWalking' and '#dogwalking' as separate tags, creating duplicates in their profile. Tags should be case-insensitive and normalized.
acceptanceCriteria: In EditProfileScreen._addTag(), normalize the tag comparison to lowercase before checking for duplicates
Store tags in their original casing but compare case-insensitively
If user tries to add a duplicate (case-insensitive), show a SnackBar: 'This skill is already added'
Trim whitespace from tags before adding
Validate tag is not empty and not just whitespace after trimming
Add minimum 2 characters, maximum 30 characters validation for tags
flutter analyze 0 errors
Write widget test: add tag 'Cooking', try to add 'cooking', verify duplicate is rejected

userStories.title: Bid Duration Field — Add Upper Bound & Better UX
userStories.description: The custom duration field in SubmitBidSheet validates only that the value is positive, but allows absurd values like 999999 minutes. The bid message field has maxLength: 200 but doesn't show a character counter. These small UX gaps confuse providers during bid submission.
acceptanceCriteria: In SubmitBidSheet, add maximum validation to custom duration: cannot exceed 1440 minutes (24 hours). Show error: 'Duration cannot exceed 24 hours'
Add a character counter below the bid message TextField showing 'X/200 characters'
When custom duration is selected, auto-focus the duration input field
Duration input: set keyboardType to number, add inputFormatter for digits only
If provider switches from custom duration back to a preset chip, clear the custom duration field
flutter analyze 0 errors

userStories.title: Replace Silent Empty Catch Blocks with User-Visible Error Feedback
userStories.description: Multiple repositories and screens have empty catch blocks (catch (_) {}) that swallow errors silently. When chat creation fails, profile updates fail, or review submission fails, the user gets no feedback — the operation just appears to do nothing.
acceptanceCriteria: Audit all catch blocks in: ChatRepository, ProfileRepository, AuthRepository, SubmitReviewScreen, BidListSection, WelcomeScreen
For each empty catch block: if the error affects user-visible behavior, re-throw or propagate the error so the calling widget can show a SnackBar
For catch blocks in repository methods called from widgets: ensure the widget layer shows an error SnackBar with a user-friendly message (not raw exception text)
For catch blocks in background operations (e.g., analytics, logging): add debugPrint() at minimum so errors are visible in dev console
Do NOT change catch blocks that intentionally swallow expected exceptions (e.g., document-not-found checks) — add a comment explaining why
flutter analyze 0 errors
